title: omega cannot use an injectivity hypothesis to relate image cardinality
informal_statement: A goal proving `(s.image f).card = s.card` given `Function.Injective f`, attacked by `omega` directly on the two `Finset.card` atoms instead of citing the structural lemma that uses the injectivity hypothesis.

failure.input_context_summary: Goal was `(s.image f).card = s.card` for `s : Finset ℕ`, `f : ℕ → ℕ`, with `hf : Function.Injective f` in context.
failure.attempt_summary: Applied `omega` directly after `intro s f hf`. `omega` cannot consume the `Function.Injective f` hypothesis at all -- it treats `s.card` and `(Finset.image f s).card` as two independent, unconstrained natural-number atoms and reports a spurious counterexample (`a - b >= 1` for `a := s.card`, `b := (Finset.image f s).card`), since it has no built-in knowledge of the image-cardinality relationship the injectivity hypothesis establishes.
failure.lesson: Bridge Finset image-cardinality facts (e.g. `Finset.card_image_of_injective`) into the goal before invoking `omega`/`nlinarith`; these tactics only see linear relations they are told about over the atoms actually present in the goal, and cannot connect an unrelated hypothesis like `Function.Injective f` to a `Finset.card` equality on their own.

notes: Produced through the tracked proof-search MCP loop (problem 8123695e-f185-49a4-8533-01f41051ae62, episode 5f59b5d8-e34b-4def-9892-5ba68f409d7b): step 1 (`intro s f hf; omega`) returned a kernel_fail with the omega tactic-failure diagnostic quoted in failure.attempt_summary; step 2, citing `Finset.card_image_of_injective` directly, reached kernel_verified in the same episode and was authored as the companion positive packet elementary.combinatorics.card_image_of_injective.v1. Closes the domain's long-queued 'omega/decide timeout on a Finset.card goal requiring an explicit structural lemma' backlog item.
